title: Security, Privacy, Reliability, and Layered Quality Evidence · Product Forensics
summary: A preserved decision record for security, privacy, reliability, and layered quality evidence contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in trust and quality assurance case and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Model assets, actors, trust boundaries, misuse, abuse, failure impact, mitigations, detection, response, and residual risk.
Trace purpose, collection, consent or authority, use, sharing, retention, access, correction, deletion, and aggregation risk.
Assign pure behavior, boundary, integration, interface, end-to-end, accessibility, security, and recovery claims to appropriate tests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Quality Nonclaim
steps.instruction:
Order the Quality Nonclaim evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Quality Nonclaim.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Quality Nonclaim assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Quality Nonclaim, keep Quality Nonclaim connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Quality Nonclaim has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Order the regression proof · Debug Quality Nonclaim, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Quality Nonclaim precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Order the regression proof · Debug Quality Nonclaim, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.

For Order the regression proof · Debug Quality Nonclaim, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A green continuous-integration run proves production quality.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints for the debug Quality Nonclaim case
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure for the debug Quality Nonclaim case
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence for the debug Quality Nonclaim case
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise for the debug Quality Nonclaim case

stimulus.title: Threat Abuse Case case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The debug variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Model assets, actors, trust boundaries, misuse, abuse, failure impact, mitigations, detection, response, and residual risk.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Security review is a vulnerability scanner run after coding.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model assets, actors, trust boundaries, misuse, abuse, failure impact, mitigations, detection, response, and residual risk. Artifact under review: trust and quality assurance case.

options.text: For inspect the failure record, apply Threat Abuse Case without its stated preconditions, omit the trust and quality assurance case trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Threat Abuse Case for trust and quality assurance case.
The record names the assumption, applies Threat Abuse Case, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Threat Abuse Case record says trust and quality assurance case looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Threat Abuse Case.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Quality Nonclaim
steps.instruction:
Append a pp1-quality-nonclaim-1vqje6: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Quality Nonclaim.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Quality Nonclaim into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Quality Nonclaim, keep Quality Nonclaim connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.code:
pp1-quality-nonclaim-1vqje6:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Quality Nonclaim structural check is only one gate. trust and quality assurance case must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Quality Nonclaim, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Quality Nonclaim precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Quality Nonclaim, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The debug variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.

For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Quality Nonclaim, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A green continuous-integration run proves production quality.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Quality Nonclaim
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Quality Nonclaim: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.

Evidence target: Publish the incident record · Debug Quality Nonclaim.
steps.why: Retrieving Quality Nonclaim through the concrete trust and quality assurance case case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Publish the incident record · Debug Quality Nonclaim, keep Quality Nonclaim connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug Quality Nonclaim, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Quality Nonclaim precondition, or invariant that must hold.
